mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to produce a negative mean return during labor market deteriorations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during late-cycle expansions signal capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during late-cycle phases generate positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of cases, invalidating the slowdown transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.3 percent signals accelerating price pressures that, in a low-rate environment, initially fuels equity momentum and positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: CPI spikes above the threshold consistently precede negative 5-day returns, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below chance levels in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between stagnant nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates labor market fragility that suppresses corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The payroll-unemployment divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with mean forward returns remaining positive during observed divergences.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2 percent confirms sustained economic expansion that supports corporate revenue trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDP growth above the 2 percent threshold yields negative 20-day forward returns in over 55% of observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during liquidity-constrained regimes trigger immediate margin calls and deleveraging, forcing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Industrial production drops exceeding 1.0 percent fail to generate negative 5-day returns, or the mechanism shows no predictive power during liquidity stress periods.
